user_prompts:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "greasy-element", "age": "65+", "sex": "Female", "sexual_orientation": "White", "demographic_group": "European", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "Some college", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Enjoys bird watching", "Active", "Democrat"]}}

logs.name: ambitious-envelope
logs.text:
>The act itself is as ineffectual as it is meaningless. No one knows who he is, or cares. Nor do they care why he tinkled on nixon. I feel like your interpreting that section a bit literally in order to avoid dealing with my actual point?

If I can't interpret you literally, how else can I understand you? "Read between the lines" is a poor method of being informed of anything.

>Yeah we do? Max headroom was played by Matt Frewer: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Matt_Frewer

I was using voice to text, it missed "broadcast interruption". My point and the previous point above both still stand.

>And yet... I'm not seeing many reagan or nixon or any family or friends of other dead folk whose graves have been desecrated shaken to the core? Or really anyone at all reacting in the manner you're suggesting. 

The point I'm constructing here it's not so much that this one act *that I have used as an example to build my case* is the height of desecration, but that regular desecration would definitely catch attention.

Again I must ask does anybody here really believe that Donald Trump would be happy knowing that people were lining up to piss on his grave?

logs.name: inverse-twig
logs.text:
>However, the power of defacing a grave cannot be ignored

Sure it can! And just like the graves themselves, those who deface them are mostly ignored.

Case and point: Your little Wee-Wee fella. Nobody knows who he is. Nobody cares what he thinks. If I did know who he was, I'd think him more than a bit childish and juvenile.

For certain, desecrating some particular graves in unique or extraordinary ways would get a short lived reaction from folks. Most people would disapprove out of some sense of respect, some might cheer the person on (but those sorts of people probably aren't of any influence or import themselves), Depending on the grave, I myself might chuckle, and others wouldn't care either way. But what is absolutely certain is that in a few days know one would think about the incident ever again unless it was specifically brought up.

The kind of psycho babble your peddling about it being a tool to those to hold people accountable reminds me of this sketch: http://www.cc.com/video-clips/zpuyqq/upright-citizens-brigade-ass-pennies

logs.name: greasy-element
logs.text: @ambitious-envelope I guess if you can't take a joke, don't come to the graveyard. And @inverse-twig, chuckling seems like a pretty good reaction to me. Maybe we should all just ignore these things and let the real issues like climate change and healthcare take center stage. Or maybe we should just chuckle and move on, because clearly, no one else cares.

logs.name: ambitious-envelope
logs.text: @greasy-element Haha, because ignoring something doesn't make it go away. And @inverse-twig, chuckling isn't exactly a sign of respect. Maybe we should all just pretend nothing happened and hope the next generation doesn't have to deal with it. Or maybe we should actually do something about it instead of just laughing it off.

logs.name: weighted-disk
logs.text: Oh wow, look at all the grown-ups getting all worked up over some silly little drawings on a grave. It's like watching paint dry, really. But hey, if it makes you feel better to lecture everyone on how they should react, go right ahead. Just remember, in a week, nobody will even remember this nonsense. Maybe then you'll stop trying to make a big deal out of nothing.

logs.name: ambitious-envelope
logs.text: @weighted-disk Seriously? Paint drying? Nice. And for the record, I'm not trying to make a big deal out of nothing. I'm just saying that maybe, just maybe, a little humor can be a healthy way to cope with serious issues. But if you prefer to wallow in drama, that's your choice.

logs.name: chilly-contract
logs.text: @weighted-disk Oh, so now it's okay to mock people who try to address real issues? Because if you can't handle a bit of sarcasm, maybe you should just stay home. Or is that too much to ask?

logs.name: inverse-twig
logs.text: @weighted-disk Ever thought about becoming a comedian? Your "humor" is about as funny as a flat tire on a rainy day. And for the record, I do remember things, unlike some people who seem to forget the value of respect.

logs.name: greasy-element
logs.text: @inverse-twig Oh, so now I'm a comedian? That's rich. Next you'll be telling me to stop being so dramatic. Just because I find humor in a situation doesn't mean I don't take things seriously. And for the record, my humor is way funnier than a flat tire. Maybe you should invest in a better sense of humor, @inverse-twig.

logs.name: weighted-disk
logs.text: @greasy-element Haha, drama queen much? At least I can laugh at myself and others without being called out. And your "sense of humor" must be top-notch since you can't see how ridiculous you sound.

logs.name: inverse-twig
logs.text: @weighted-disk @greasy-element Seriously, can we just agree to disagree and maybe not tear each other down while doing it? Or is that too much to ask for around here?

logs.name: weighted-disk
logs.text: @inverse-twig Sometimes I wonder if we're even talking about the same thing. But sure, let's pretend we can all just get along and not have any fun. Maybe next time you could try laughing at something other than yourself though, @inverse-twig.

logs.name: inverse-twig
logs.text: @weighted-disk I guess when you're so set on winning every argument, losing sight of the actual topic becomes a small price to pay. Next time, maybe focus on the conversation instead of the victory. And yeah, maybe I'll save my self-deprecating humor for when it actually fits.

logs.name: weighted-disk
logs.text: @inverse-twig Oh, come on, @inverse-twig! You're making it way too serious. Let's just have a good laugh at how passionate we both are—about nothing, really. 😂 #argumentover

logs.name: chilly-contract
logs.text: @weighted-disk Haha, you must really enjoy the drama, @weighted-disk. It's like watching paint dry, but with more yelling. Next time, how 'bout we actually talk about something interesting? #argumentover #boring

logs.name: national-blockade
logs.text: @chilly-contract Paint drying with more yelling? That's a new one. But hey, if we're going for boring, let's do it right. Maybe we can all just agree to disagree and watch paint dry together. #argumentover #boringfast

logs.name: chilly-contract
logs.text: @national-blockade Sounds like a plan, @national-blockade. Let's all just sit here and watch the world pass us by while we argue about nothing. #argumentover #boringfast
